title: All Animals Are Equal #2
description: Opening | Khai mạc: 02.11.2019 @ 4:00pm-10:00pm Event duration | Thời gian sự kiện: 03.11.2019 – 06.11.2019 @ 2:00pm-6:00pm Location | Địa điểm: A. Farm @ 96/1 Tan Thoi Nhat 6, D.12, HCMC The inaugural All Animals Are Equal took place in 2018 as the opening event to A. Farm, transitioning the space from its role as factory warehouse for scent and flavour exportation, and reimagining it as an international platform for artists to live and work together. Now in its second edition, All Animals Are Equal is an open call to ANY and ALL artists working in or connected to Ho Chi Minh City (Saigon). Artists are invited to bring one to three artworks to A. Farm in a self-curated group hang, with space allocated on a first-come, first-served basis. Video artists are included in a dedicated screening room, with sound and performance artists also featured in the event. Conceived by two of A. Farm’s founders, Dinh Q. Le and Cam Xanh, All Animals Are Equal is a chance for first-time artists to show alongside their more established contemporaries, with artists taking over the entire A. Farm in a snapshot of Saigon’s current creative moment. Artists are offered the opportunity to sell their works from All Animals Are Equal with A. Farm. Sales are split ‘equally’ 60% to the artist and 40% to A. Farm. All funds received by A. Farm contribute to the ongoing running and longevity of the project. …….. All Animals Are Equal (Muôn Loài Bình Đẳng) phiên bản đầu đã diễn ra vào năm 2018, cũng là sự kiện mở đầu cho A. Farm, một không gian chuyển đổi từ nhà máy sản xuất hương liệu sang không gian quốc tế nơi các nghệ sĩ sống và làm việc cùng nhau. Phiên bản thứ hai của All Animals Are Equal là lời mời mở tới BẤT CỨ và TẤT CẢ các nghệ sĩ nào làm việc tại, hoặc có kết nối với, Thành phố Hồ Chí Minh. Các nghệ sĩ được mời mang 1-3 tác phẩm nghệ thuật đến A. Farm, tham dự một triển lãm nhóm ‘tự quản,’ với không gian được phân bổ trên cơ sở ai đến trước được phục vụ trước. Các nghệ sĩ video trưng bày trong một phòng chiếu chuyên dụng, và các nghệ sĩ âm thanh và trình diễn cũng góp mặt trong sự kiện này. Ý tưởng của chương trình được hình thành bởi hai trong số những người sáng lập của A. Farm, Đỉnh Q. Lê và Cam Xanh, All Animals Are Equal là cơ hội cho các nghệ sĩ trẻ trưng bày cùng những nghệ sĩ đương thời có kinh nghiệm hơn; các nghệ sĩ được tiếp quản toàn bộ không gian A. Farm trong một khoảnh khắc sáng tạo của Sài Gòn. Các nghệ sĩ có cơ hội bán tác phẩm ở All Animals Are Equal, hỗ trợ bởi A. Farm. Doanh số được chia ’bình đẳng,’ 60% cho nghệ sĩ và 40% cho A. Farm. Tất cả khoản phí mà A. Farm nhận được đều góp phần vào quá trình vận hành lâu dài của không gian. All Animals Are Equal #2 participating artists / All Animals Are Equal #2 gồm các nghệ sĩ Alisa Chunchue Nguyễn Đức Phương (Phuong Gio) Maung Day Tram Carin Luong Kim Duy Lê Phi Long Oliver Dicostanzo Nguyễn Việt Cương Tuyển Nguyễn Lan Anh Lê Nguyễn Quốc Dũng John Edmond Smyth Josie Rae Turnbull Đinh Quang Anh Nguyễn Võ

title: Matteo Biella
description: Matteo Biella graduated as an architect from TU Delft, the Netherlands, in 2015, and his scope of work has recently blended into art. He recycles wood from old and broken skateboard decks to create artworks that are inspired by his architectural research. Matteo has previously carried out Artist-In-Residence programs and presented his works in Chengdu, Shanghai, Sapporo & Tokyo Learn more about his works: matteobiella.com Matteo Biella – MoT+++ A. Farm International Art Residency a Saigon-based art residency, in partnership with the Goethe-Institut Ho Chi Minh City, and support from Amanaki Thao Dien

title: Tina Thu
description: Tina Thu is a Chinese artist born in Long Khanh, Vietnam. She immigrated to Lawrence, Massachusetts at three-months-old and later studied Printmaking at Massachusetts College of Art and Design in Boston. Inspired by her family and their practice of ancestral veneration, her work is an attempt to illustrate childhood dreams of hellscapes. Through the process of mapping out this imaginary land, she tries to collect and research displacement of her family’s culture. These grounds are a space for her to reconnect past the loss of tradition and history. Learn more about her works: tinathu.com Tina Thu – MoT+++ A. Farm International Art Residency a Saigon-based art residency, in partnership with the Goethe-Institut Ho Chi Minh City, and support from Amanaki Thao Dien

title: Kanich Khajohnsri
description: From the artist: ‘At the beginning of my practice, I have been thinking about space, demarcations, and overlaps between spaces. Through observations carried out in Bangkok, I have noticed the ambiguity that resulted from the overlaps between private and public spaces. Now, I want to learn more in ranges of the idea about the ambiguity of a physical space, whether it be landscape, area, place. that I can see, hear, or even sense from the area. This includes the theoretical scape and non-physical such as theory, story, rumor, folklore, and other kinds of oral history, that I encountered with. I want to know how these fragments are connected together creating characteristic of the “Place”.’ Khajohnsri graduated from the King Mongkut’s Institute of Technology Ladkrabang department of Fine Arts in 2016, he got the Young Thai Artists Award for Photography the same year. He has exhibited his work in both Thailand and Germany. Kanich Khajohnsri – MoT+++ A. Farm International Art Residency a Saigon-based art residency, in partnership with the Goethe-Institut Ho Chi Minh City, and support from Amanaki Thao Dien

title: Levi Masuli
description: A writer by profession, Levi Masuli used to play jazz piano in hotels and cafes before deciding to look for more ways to create sound. He now explores its conceptual possibilities. With hyperproduction and saturation of content defining the current creative zeitgeist across fields and genres, particularly in music and sound, the artist aims not just to create more sound but also to challenge existing modes of listening. Learn more at: levi-masuli.com Levi Masuli – MoT+++ A. Farm International Art Residency a Saigon-based art residency, in partnership with the Goethe-Institut Ho Chi Minh City, and support from Amanaki Thao Dien

title: Lập Xuân
description: Lập Xuân (Do Nguyen Lap-Xuan) is a Ho Chi Minh City-based artist who participated in performance plus 2019 at MoT+++.

title: performance plus 2019 | tuesday movie night
description: after hours fun: performance art movies, documentaries and informal discussions every Tuesday night at MoT+++. follow our facebook page for content update. session #1 T uesday, November 26 7:00 pm

title: performance plus 2019 | spoken words
description: long before writing, oral tradition conveyed information. rhythm, rhymes, repetition and attitude transform words into performance art medium. poetry readings, poetry slams, jazz poetry, prose, monologues… if you have what to say and want to join spoken words November 28 7:00 pm

title: Edén Barrena
description: Edén Barrena (Badajoz, Spain, 1991) is a visual artist working with drawing. Her academic training consists of a Fine Arts BA from the Universidad Complutense de Madrid, and a Printmaking MFA from the Royal College of Art, London. She did one year of her undergraduate studies at the Staatliche Akademie der Bildenden Künste Stuttgart, Germany, thanks to an Erasmus scholarship. She was a recipient of the Villiers Travel Award (2014) and the artist-in-residence fellowships from the Fundación Antonio Gala (Córdoba, ES, 2015-16) and the Swatch Art Peace Hotel (Shanghai, CN, 2017). In 2018, Edén was invited by Bananafish Books, Shanghai, to publish her artist book The Promise of Blooming. During her residency at A. Farm, Edén worked on her project An Altar of Touching, in which she will research the poetics of offerings realised by people’s religious beliefs ascribed to Hinduism and Buddhism in Vietnam, Thailand and India. Edén Barrena – MoT+++ A. Farm International Art Residency a Saigon-based art residency, in partnership with the Goethe-Institut Ho Chi Minh City, and support from Amanaki Thao Dien

title: studio | Cian Duggan
description: Cian Duggan studio @ MoT+++ Sep 18 – Nov 10, 2019 opening: Friday October 25, 2019 @ 7pm on view: October 26 – November 10, 2019 hours: Tuesday to Saturday, 2-6pm MoT+++ collective member Cian Duggan will use the gallery space as his studio from September 18 – November 10, 2019. during this time he will develop a body of work using materials new to his practice. his studio will open to the public from 7pm on Friday October 25, 2019. please message us before this if you would like to visit. Cian is the fourth member of the collective to occupy the MoT+++ space. about the artist: Cian Duggan (b.1990, Ireland) renders the familiar unfamiliar in his painting and drawing practice, which combines organic yet alien shapes with human and figurative elements. the results are abstract forms that appear otherworldly, and hover as if animated with static tension. after working at an artist residency in Ireland, Cian moved first to London and then Ho Chi Minh City, Vietnam in 2017. his works can be found on streets and walls across various cities worldwide and often are often in dialogue with a specific site or location. Cian reverses this process in his studio practice by using found objects or materials, such as metal or wood, as his canvas, responding instead to the nature of the material. in 2018, Cian joined the A. Farm international art residency as a Season 0 artist, and became a member of the MoT+++ collective.

title: rainy season | mùa mưa | Kim Duy
description: rainy season | mùa mưa Kim Duy opening: Thursday, September 12, 2019 @ 7pm on view: July 15 – September 14, 2019 MoT+++ collective member Kim Duy presented new works developed over the course of his two months occupation of the MoT+++ space. Kim Duy’s studio is the third in a series of occupations of the gallery space from the MoT+++ collective. the exploration of individual subjectivity as the nexus for meaning resonates throughout Kim Duy’s studio. his resulting works detail a journey away from the possibility of objectivity, establishing a distance informed by both western critical theory and eastern Buddhist teaching, and excavates instead a notion of being that is relational and interdependent. they reject a trend for participation from the audience in order to situate the mind, rather than body, as the key mediator of experience. together they provide a kaleidoscopic view of shifting perceptions that locate the individual and its position in the current moment as the lens of interpretation through which meaning is generated. using the works not as a tool but rather a stimulus, Duy invites his audience to contemplate the construct of the self through concepts of language, relativity, truth and understanding. about the artist: through a multidisciplinary practice, Kim Duy questions the reading of history through visual art practice and the performative nature existing behind still images. his work often uses modest materials, such as paper, to explore interests in minimalism, experimentation and abstraction. Kim Duy was born in Hanoi, Vietnam in 1987. he studied painting at the University of Fine Arts, Hanoi from 2005 to 2008 before moving to Germany in 2009. he graduated in 2014 from the Academy of Fine Arts, Nuremberg. in 2018 Kim Duy was selected as an A. Farm resident artist for the July to December season in Ho Chi Minh City, Vietnam and joined the MoT+++ artist collective. his work can be found in the Post Vidai and the Nguyen Art Foundation collections. with thanks to Post Vidai Collection for the loan of the work ‘Field of shredded paper’ (2017). download the booklet: rainy season | mùa mưa | Kim Duy studio rainy season | mùa mưa | Kim Duy – MoT+++ programs collective MoTsound A. Farm about us rainy season | mùa mưa | Kim Duy rainy season | mùa mưa Kim Duy opening: Thursday, September 12, 2019 @ 7pm on view: July 15 – September 14, 2019 MoT+++ collective member Kim Duy presented new works developed over the course of his two months occupation of the MoT+++ space. title: Rachel TonThat
description: Rachel TonThat is a film photographer, multimedia artist, and fiction writer living in Hanoi, Vietnam. Influenced by her childhood dream of becoming an explorer, her current photographic work documents moments from the ongoing story of climate change and human development in a quickly transforming world. Her stories draw from folk tales, myth, and oral storytelling. Rachel is the co-author and co-creator of the artist book Lost Cities. She graduated from Eugene Lang College and Parsons School of Design in New York. Learn more about her works: racheltonthat.com Rachel TonThat – MoT+++ A. Farm International Art Residency a Saigon-based art residency, in partnership with the Goethe-Institut Ho Chi Minh City, and support from Amanaki Thao Dien

title: rainy season | mùa mưa | Kim Duy studio
description: from September 6-14, 2019, MoT+++ collective member Kim Duy presented new works developed over the course of his two months occupation of the MoT+++ space. Kim Duy’s studio is the third in a series of occupations of the gallery space from the MoT+++ collective. the exploration of individual subjectivity as the nexus for meaning resonates throughout Kim Duy’s studio. his resulting works detail a journey away from the possibility of objectivity, establishing a distance informed by both western critical theory and eastern Buddhist teaching, and excavates instead a notion of being that is relational and interdependent. they reject a trend for participation from the audience in order to situate the mind, rather than body, as the key mediator of experience. together they provide a kaleidoscopic view of shifting perceptions that locate the individual and its position in the current moment as the lens of interpretation through which meaning is generated. using the works not as a tool but rather a stimulus, Duy invites his audience to contemplate the construct of the self through concepts of language, relativity, truth and understanding. about the artist: through a multidisciplinary practice, Kim Duy questions the reading of history through visual art practice and the performative nature existing behind still images. his work often uses modest materials, such as paper, to explore interests in minimalism, experimentation and abstraction. title: MoT sound #11 | aDom
description: MoT+++ is very proud to present a very special happening – the first-ever ensemble appearance of Dom Dom experimental music collective in Saigon! Dom Dom Center for Experimental Music was formed in Hanoi in 2012 by contemporary composer/arranger and multi-instrumental improvisational sound artist/performer Tran Kim Ngoc. since its establishment, Dom Dom collective and the artists who have passed through its experimental improvisation courses stand out as one of the single largest contributors to the Vietnamese contemporary sound art scene. exploring a variety of mediums and modes, Dom Dom’s impressive roster of artists have explored worlds of acoustic and electronic sound to an impressive depth, making a mark on the international stage, and continuing to put Vietnam on the world map of contemporary sound art. join us on August 14th for a very special performance including new and old members of the Dom Dom collective, led by founder “chị đại” Kim Ngoc, a giant of the Vietnamese contemporary music scene, organizer of the Hanoi New Music Festival, named by Forbes as one of the 50 Most Influential Women in Vietnam 2019 . for one night only, a unique journey through worlds of sound and improvisation delving deep into mind and space, wandering through galaxies of imagination in a singular exploration of the pure language of expression. Wednesday, August 14 8:00 pm
vnDescription: thắp 1 tia Đom Đóm in Ho Chi Minh City. MộT+++ sự kiện được chờ đón nhất trong mùa thu này, sẽ là Một Đom Đóm trong lòng Sài Gòn! theo lời mời của MoT+++, vào ngày 14/08, nhà soạn nhạc đa phương tiện và nghệ sĩ trình diễn ngẫu hứng nhạc thể nghiệm Kim Ngọc sẽ cùng các nhạc sĩ trẻ tài năng- thành viên của khoá trình diễn ngẫu hứng nhạc thể nghiệm Đom Đóm (khoá học do đích thân chị hướng dẫn) đến với cộng đồng yêu nhạc thể nghiệm Sài Gòn. màn “chào sân gay cấn” này sẽ bao gồm các tiết mục ngẫu hứng ý niệm và ngẫu hứng tự do khác nhau của các nhạc sĩ trẻ với sự đồng hành của “chị đại” Kim Ngọc. thắp một Tia Đom Đóm” sẽ gợi mở, đưa dẫn khán giả tiến vào một vũ trụ âm thanh tưởng chừng như hỗn độn, nhưng thực chất chứa đựng đầy đủ cả vẻ đẹp lẫn những trải nghiệm văn hoá phi tuyến tính. hãy cùng DomDom mở toang các giác quan với thế giới âm thanh, với trí tưởng tượng để rong chơi cùng âm thanh, cùng sự tự do của ngôn ngữ biểu đạt. hẹn một gặp gỡ đầu tiên tại Sài Gòn nhé. Thứ tư, 14 tháng 08 8:00 pm địa điểm: Saigon Domaine, Tầng trệt, 1057 Bình Quoi, Phường 28, quận Bình Thạnh, thành phố Hồ Chí Minh. vé vào cửa: 150.000 đ musicians Trần Khánh Ly/ vocal, guitar Tuấn Nị/ violin Hoài Anh/ tranh Lê Duy/ bamboo flute, jew harp Hà Thuý Hằng/ tranh&objectsBông Hoa/ violin&objects Kim Ngọc/ piano, vocal visual artist Cao Hoang Long with special guest Alec Schachner/ trumpet, percussion trong khoảng Đom Đóm Đom Đóm được thành lập năm 2012 với vai trò là một trung tâm nghệ thuật độc lập đầu tiên tại Việt Nam cống hiến cho các hoạt động phát triển âm nhạc thể nghiệm và sự cộng tác liên ngành của nó với các lĩnh vực nghệ thuật thể nghiệm khác. được hình thành với mô hình độc lập phi lợi nhuận, sứ mệnh của Đom Đóm là nuôi dưỡng thế hệ nối tiếp cho âm nhạc thể nghiệm Viêt, đồng thời thúc đẩy các thực hành âm nhạc và nghệ thuật thể nghiệm đương đại Việt Nam. nhạc sĩ Trần Kim Ngọc – founder của Đom Đóm, người trực tiếp hướng dẫn các khóa học thường được các bạn học viên Đom Đóm và giới nghệ sĩ yêu mến gọi là “chị đại”- Với giải thưởng cao quý là một trong 50 người phụ nữ có ảnh hưởng nhất Việt Nam năm 2019 do Tạp chí Forbes bình chọn, nhạc sĩ Kim Ngọc đã khẳng định vị thế của mình là một trong số các nhạc sĩ đầu tiên mang âm nhạc đương đại, thể nghiệm về Việt Nam đồng thời tạo ra một “ngữ pháp” bản địa cho loại hình nghệ thuật này thông qua các tác phẩm đã được quốc tế biết tới của mình. với nền tảng thành tựu cá nhân vững vàng, chị đã thành lập Đom Đóm năm 2012 và là người khởi xướng các chương trình giảng dạy Âm nhạc thể nghiệm đương đại, tạo ra một thế hệ mới các nghệ sĩ trẻ hiện đang ngày một đông hơn và trở thành những nhân tố đa dạng, tích cực nhất trong khung cảnh âm nhạc thể nghiệm Việt Nam. việc tổ chức Liên hoan Nhạc mới tại Việt Nam – Hanoi New Music Festival, ba kỳ liên hoan đã được tổ chức vào 2009, 2013, 2018 là những nỗ lực không ngừng nghỉ để Đom Đóm có thể đưa được âm nhạc đương đại Việt Nam và Đông Nam Á có tên trên bản đồ âm nhạ

title: Nguyen Le Phuong Linh
description: Born in Vietnam, where she spent most of her youth, Nguyen Le Phuong Linh relocated to France to pursue studies at the Ecole de beaux-arts de Nîmes, from where she graduated in 2016. Based in the Gard region in the south of France, where she has lived since then, she works as a photographer, drawing inspiration from the landscapes, ever-evolving natural terrain, or even more so, from the rituals of France and Vietnam in which she identifies commonalities and distinctions that appear in her work. Not only working in the photographic medium, she also collects objects of all sorts. She periodically creates installations which embody a form of poetic archaeology resembling the environments where she lives. Nguyen Le Phuong Linh’s residency is part of the Villa Saigon x A. Farm collaboration. Nguyen Le Phuong Linh – MoT+++ A. Farm International Art Residency a Saigon-based art residency, in partnership with the Goethe-Institut Ho Chi Minh City, and support from Amanaki Thao Dien

title: Weston Teruya
description: Weston Teruya is an Oakland, California-based, Honolulu, Hawai’i-born artist who has exhibited at the Mills College Art Museum (Oakland), Yerba Buena Center for the Arts (San Francisco), Kearny Street Workshop (SF), University of Hawai’i, Mānoa (Honolulu), Longhouse Projects & the NYC Fire Museum (New York), the Atlanta Contemporary Art Center, and Hiromi Yoshii (Tokyo). He has received public art commissions from the San Francisco and Alameda County Arts Commissions. He has received grants from Artadia, Asian Cultural Council, Creative Work Fund, and the Center for Cultural Innovation. Weston has been an artist-in-residence at Montalvo Arts Center, Ox-Bow, the deYoung Museum, Recology SF, and Kala Art Institute. Along with his individual practice, he is one-third of the collective, Related Tactics, and produces and hosts (un)making, a podcast through the West Coast US online arts writing and criticism publication, Art Practical. Weston received his MFA in Painting & Drawing and MA in Visual & Critical Studies from California College of the Arts and BA in Studio Art with a minor in Asian American Studies from Pomona College. Weston’s residency was supported by the Asian Cultural Council . Learn more about his works: westonteruya.com Weston Teruya – MoT+++ A. Farm International Art Residency a Saigon-based art residency, in partnership with the Goethe-Institut Ho Chi Minh City, and support from Amanaki Thao Dien

title: MoT Doi Gai | A Beach Life | Cam Xanh
description: MoT Doi Gai | A Beach Life Cam Xanh opening: Saturday June 22, 2019 @ 6.30pm on view: June 22 – July 5, 2019 often known as the world’s oldest profession, Cam Xanh’s MoT Doi Gai | A Beach Life explores the theme of prostitution at a conceptual level. starting with a quote from Gore Vidal that “we are all prostitutes in one sense or another, ethically if not sexually”, the exhibition goes on to investigate the contemporary relationships between feminism, love and sexuality, art, beauty, power, economics, and the potential for exploitation that emerges at their intersections. MoT Doi Gai | A Beach Life is the culmination of Cam Xanh’s close studio in the MoT+++ space. Cam Xanh is the second in a series of occupations of the gallery space from the MoT+++ collective. about the artist: Cam Xanh is the pseudonym of conceptual artist Tran Thanh Ha (b.1977, Vietnam). translated as Green Orange from Vietnamese, it is the first of many playful departures from the expected that characterise her work. her physical works are often based on texts and poetry, or developed from previous performances by the artist. they take on many forms such as installation, sculpture, painting and video, and often include elements only activated by audience participation. in 2016 she was a speaker for the ASIA YOUNG 36 exhibition at the Jeonbuk Museum of Art in Seoul, Korea and founded independent art space MoT+++ in 2015 in Ho Chi Minh City, Vietnam. in 2018 she co-founded A. Farm, an international art residency, to further support the growth of the Vietnamese art scene. her work can be found in the collections of the Nguyen Art Foundation and the Singapore Art Museum (SAM). download the booklet: MoT Doi Gai | A Beach Life | Cam Xanh MoT Doi Gai | A Beach Life | Cam Xanh – MoT+++ programs collective MoTsound A. Farm about us MoT Doi Gai | A Beach Life | Cam Xanh

title: Roberto Sifuentes
description: Roberto Sifuentes is an interdisciplinary performance artist and educator. Sifuente’s recent projects include: #exsanguination, a collaboration with new media artist jonCates and Aram Han Sifuentes (2016-2019); “Reculmulations: Digital Avatars and Performance objects” A collaboration with digital artist Claudia Hart and composer Edmund Campion (Black and White Gallery NYC). As, co-founder of the San Francisco based performance troupe La Pocha Nostra. He has performed and conducted workshops with La Pocha across the US, Canada, Europe and Latin America. Sifuentes has co-authored two books with Guilermo Gomez-Peña; most recently “Exercises for Rebel Artists: Radical Performance Pedagogy” Routledge 2011. As a performance pedagogue, he has been Artistic Director of the Trinity College/La MaMa Performing Arts Program NYC. Sifuentes is currently Professor of performance at the School of the Art Institute of Chicago. Roberto’s residency was supported by the Asian Cultural Council . Learn more about his works: robertosifuentes.com Roberto Sifuentes – MoT+++ A. Farm International Art Residency a Saigon-based art residency, in partnership with the Goethe-Institut Ho Chi Minh City, and support from Amanaki Thao Dien

title: Aram Han Sifuentes
description: Aram Han Sifuentes is a fiber and performance artist who works to claim spaces for immigrant and disenfranchised communities. Her work often revolves around skill sharing, specifically sewing techniques, to create multiethnic and intergenerational sewing circles, which become a place for empowerment, subversion and protest. Her works have been exhibited at Pulitzer Arts Foundation (St. Louis, MO), Jane Addams Hull-House Museum (Chicago, IL), Hyde Park Art Center (Chicago, IL), Chicago Cultural Center (Chicago, IL), Asian Arts Initiative (Philadelphia, PA), and the Chung Young Yang Embroidery Museum (Seoul, South Korea). Aram is a 2016 Smithsonian Artist Research Fellow, 2016 3Arts Awardee, and 2017 Sustainable Arts Foundation Awardee. Her project Protest Banner Lending Library was a finalist for the Beazley Design Awards at the Design Museum (London, UK) in 2016. She earned her BA in Art and Latin American Studies from the University of California, Berkeley, and her MFA in Fiber and Material Studies from the School of the Art Institute of Chicago. She is currently an Adjunct Associate Professor at the School of the Art Institute of Chicago. Aram’s residency was supported by the Asian Cultural Council . Learn more about her works: aramhansifuentes.com Aram Han Sifuentes – MoT+++ A. Farm International Art Residency a Saigon-based art residency, in partnership with the Goethe-Institut Ho Chi Minh City, and support from Amanaki Thao Dien

title: studio | Kim Duy
description: from July 15, 2019 collective member Kim Duy will begin his occupation of the MoT+++ space. over the course of two months Kim Duy will develop new works and invites the audience to observe his ongoing art marking processes. his studio will last until September 19, 2019. Kim Duy’s open studio is the third in a series of occupations of the gallery space from the MoT+++ collective. please message us if you would like to visit. about the artist: through a multidisciplinary practice, Kim Duy questions the changing conditions of time and space in art and language, the reading of history through visual art and the performative nature existing behind still images. his artworks use modest materials such as paper to explore his interests in minimalism, experimentation and abstraction. Kim Duy was born in Hanoi, Vietnam in 1987. he studied painting at the University of Fine Arts, Hanoi from 2005 to 2008 before moving to Germany in 2010. he graduated in 2014 from the Academy of Fine Arts, Nuremberg, where he was under the instruction of Professor Jochen Flinzer who awarded him Meisterschüler. in 2018 Kim Duy was selected as an A. Farm residency artist for the July to December season in Ho Chi Minh City, Vietnam and joined the MoT+++ artist collective. his work can be found in the Post Vidai and the Nguyen Art Foundation collections.

title: MoT Doi Gai | A Beach Life | Cam Xanh
description: MoT Doi Gai | A Beach Life Cam Xanh opening: Saturday June 22, 2019 @ 6.30pm on view: June 22 – July 5, 2019 often known as the world’s oldest profession, Cam Xanh’s MoT Doi Gai | A Beach Life explores the theme of prostitution at a conceptual level. starting with a quote from Gore Vidal that “we are all prostitutes in one sense or another, ethically if not sexually”, the exhibition goes on to investigate the contemporary relationships between feminism, love and sexuality, art, beauty, power, economics, and the potential for exploitation that emerges at their intersections.

title: Natalia Ludmila
description: Natalia Ludmila is an artist who works at the intersection of painting, drawing, video and sound. Her practice is studio-based research centered on problematising the portrayal of certain social issues. In order to question forms of representation and the construction of false or biased narratives. In her research Ludmila looks for the possibility of different and multiple readings. Articulated through as diverse views and experiences as there are viewers. Learn more about her works: natalialudmila.net Natalia Ludmila – MoT+++ A. Farm International Art Residency a Saigon-based art residency, in partnership with the Goethe-Institut Ho Chi Minh City, and support from Amanaki Thao Dien

title: Lan Anh Lê
description: A. Farm International Art Residency — March to June 2019 Lan Anh Lê (1993–2020, Vietnam) was a multidisciplinary artist focusing on oral history, mythology, and public cultures of memory. She investigated relationships between mythology, ecological formations, and diverse cultural expressions of cosmic relationships. She earned a BA in Ethnicity Studies from Stanford University in 2015 before relocating to Ho Chi Minh City. For Lananh, artmaking was a psychological healing tool and her own meditative process, highly linked to ancestral worship and the search for spiritual order in a world full of chaos. Her practice lay between multimedia experimentation and art therapy, aiming to heal cynical disillusions through fantastical reveries and magical escapisms.

title: A. Farm | solo-sans-solo
description: A. Farm Season 1 Festival As we near the end of Season 1 and the one-year anniversary when our residency and collaborative experiments all began, A. Farm would like to invite you to solo-sans-solo, an end of season festival celebrating the projects developed by our artists over the last five months. The event will feature works by Tuyen Nguyen, Karen Thao La, Alisa Chunchue and Scott Anderson. Newly-arrived artist Natalia Ludmila will present her current research in an open-studio format, while Lem Trag will use her studio space as site for performance. Finally, invited Swiss artists Studio z1 will create an immersive sound experience in the gallery in a final performance to end the evening. Additional lures range from carefully-curated cocktails from 289e, to karaoke battles on our main stage, so come celebrate with us in full glory! Opening: Saturday May 18, 2019 from 5 – 10:30pm Exhibition on view: Sunday, May 19 – Wednesday, May 22 (inclusive) from 11am – 7pm Location: A. Farm | 96/1 Tan Thoi Nhat 6, D.12, HCMC Free entrance Established in 2018, A. Farm is a hub for artists, cultural workers, events and exhibitions. Located in a converted factory at the edge of Ho Chi Minh City, A. Farm is collaboratively managed by the arts organizations Nguyen Art Foundation , MoT+++ and Sàn Art

title: close studio | Cam Xanh
description: MoT+++ will host the close studio of Cam Xanh until Friday July 5, 2019. we welcome visitors most days, please message us prior to arrange. / facebook: @motplusplusplus /

title: z1 studio
description: Combining their personal experiences and research in sound, light and space, Camille de Dieu and Laurent Novac created Studio Z1, a digital performative studio, in 2016. Over the last decade, Camille de Dieu has focused her work on matters and its reaction to different contexts using a range of techniques and mediums such as projection mapping, 3D modeling, sculpture, and light projection. Laurent Novac’s research is primarily situated in the creation of tools and works of “live” performance, with a particular accent on graphics rendering and sound creation. Over the last ten years, he participated in the development of audiovisual installations and performances that have found worldwide resonance. Learn more about their works: xy-z1.xyz z1 studio – MoT+++ A. Farm International Art Residency a Saigon-based art residency, in partnership with the Goethe-Institut Ho Chi Minh City, and support from Amanaki Thao Dien

title: Constance Meffre
description: Trained in both Art History, and Cultural & Exhibition Management, she has also studied Visual Arts and Music in France, the Netherlands and Japan. In 2010, Meffre moves to Marseille where she founds D.D.A (Diffusing Digital Art), a curatorial and artistic platform for new media and sound art. There she develops activities to support artists with the production of artworks, curatorial exhibitions and residencies, and works in collaboration with different professionals in France and abroad. In 2016 she is invited to curate several exhibitions at Art-cade-Galerie des Grands Bains Douches in Marseille. This residency was made possible with the support of the D.D.A. Constance Meffre – MoT+++ A. Farm International Art Residency a Saigon-based art residency, in partnership with the Goethe-Institut Ho Chi Minh City, and support from Amanaki Thao Dien

title: Mariana Tubio-Blasio
description: Mariana Tubio-Blasio is a writer and researcher from Brazil. During her residency, she hopes to document and report the projects and daily creative processes of artists at A. Farm and in the region with a particular interest in new expressions and fresh cultural productions in Vietnam. The visual and testimonial material will be incorporated in Amor Fati, a documentary that explores the bias of art production and the diverse modes of self-expression as a powerful personal and social transformation tool in post-conflict countries. Mariana Tubio-Blasio – MoT+++ A. Farm International Art Residency a Saigon-based art residency, in partnership with the Goethe-Institut Ho Chi Minh City, and support from Amanaki Thao Dien

title: Cam Xanh | run run run | ongoing collaborative performance
description: run run run is a collaborative and ongoing performance that seizes the value of coincidence and spontaneity to playfully interrogate the worldwide phenomenon of the art fair. the performance, initiated by Cam Xanh, was originally devised to last the duration of her visit to Hong Kong for Art Basel. it responds to the pressures put on artists by an ever increasing frequency of fairs and biennials, further satirising the ubiquity of the international art fair model and exhausting homogeneity of its viewing experience. the performance commenced on Tuesday April 26, 2019, after which multiple individuals became, at times wittingly and unwittingly, running conspirators. capitalising on the serendipity of coinciding opening nights, those from MoT+++ remaining in Vietnam simultaneously attended the opening of local fair Art For You. both articulations sparked further unprompted and impulsive sprints from others—the performance’s rhizomatic spread an unexpected mirroring of the art fair’s self-replicating spread across cities and continents. the concurrent uploads to the @motplusplusplus Instagram story, with continuing textual deviations from Cam Xanh, subsequently form a playful echo, reverberating between countries to produce a live update from the global art world. Cam Xanh | run run run | ongoing collaborative performance – MoT+++ programs collective MoTsound A. Farm about us Cam Xanh | run run run | ongoing collaborative performance
vnTitle: Cam Xanh | run run run | trình diễn tiếp nối và kết hợp
vnDescription: run run run là một trình diễn tiếp nối và kết hợp nắm bắt giá trị của trùng khớp và ngẫu nhiên nhằm đùa nghịch chất vấn hiện tượng của hội chợ nghệ thuật. cuộc trình diễn, khởi xướng bởi Cam Xanh, được bày ra để vượt qua khoảng thời gian của chuyến viếng thăm Hong Kong cho hội chợ Art Basel. nó phản hồi cho sự đè nén áp đặt lên nghệ sỹ bởi tần suất ngày càng tăng của hội chợ và triển lãm lưỡng niên (biennals), đồng thời châm biếm tính muôn nơi hiện hình của hình thái hội chợ nghệ thuật quốc tế và trải nghiệm chán ngắt của sự đơn điệu từ việc chiêm ngưỡng chúng. trình diễn bắt đầu vào Thứ ba 26 tháng Tư, 2019, sau việc một loạt các cá thể trở thành những người chạy âm mưu, tự nguyện hoặc không tự nguyện. tận dụng sự ngẫu nhiên của các đêm khai mạc trùng hợp, những thành viên của MoT+++ còn lại tại Việt Nam đồng thời cũng tham dự buổi khai mạc của hội chợ nghệ thuật bản địa Art For You. hai sự trùng khớp này làm loé lên những thôi thúc của các cuộc chạy nước rút tự giác từ những người khác. Tính thân rễ của trình diễn đã tạo nên một sự soi chiếu lặp lại của tự động tái tạo của các hội chợ nghệ thuật đang diễn ra trên các thành phố và lục địa. những định dạng đồng thời được tải lên trang Instagram story của @motplusplusplus, cùng với những sai lệch văn bản tiếp diễn từ Cam Xanh, tiếp sau hình thành thành tiếng vọng đùa cợt, phản chiếu giữa những quốc gia để tạo tác một bản tường thuật trực tiếp từ thế giới nghệ thuật toàn cầu. Cam Xanh | run run run | trình diễn tiếp nối và kết hợp – MoT+++ programs collective MoTsound A. Farm about us Cam Xanh | run run run | trình diễn tiếp nối và kết hợp

title: MoT sound #10 | aNoise
description: MoT sound reaches double-digits! the second MoT sound instalment of 2019, aNoise explored a variety of approaches to noise art – an eclectic mix of artists from different backgrounds and practices who somehow share similar textures in their work. Arnont Nongyao incorporates a variety of DIY creations into a Calder-esque feedback looping machine working between audiovisual elements, physical movement, light triggered sound, sound triggered light, conjuring a mini worldscape of experience. • • • has spent many years delving deep into the possibilities of coding as a sound design tool, building his own virtual environments to create a breed of harsh noise with an always unique, ever-emerging complexity. Huong DoNa is a young sound artist based in Hanoi who explores objects as soundscape creating devices, employing a variety of materials to explore a deep range of acoustic textures. more on the artists: Arnont Nongyao Arnont Nongyao has been interested and research into sound with concentration on vibration, so most of his works are differently experimental and relative to vibration searching for the value of vibration derived from connected things, such as human beings, objects and society. his works are involved in a specific space and audience’s participation. they are also connected with the mode of listening/hearing in a social situation, and with how people interact with and participate in sound. Arnont is Co-Director of CMC (Chiang Mai Collective) with Thatchatham Silsupan, that is a group of sound artists & musician working on sound art & experimental music in Chiangmai. arnontnongyao.com • • • for the last decade, the being behind the soundless moniker • • • a.k.a. Nikola H. Mounoud processes analog, digital and hybrid feedbacks in real time to produce unique, powerful and highly dynamic live performances where irresponsible noise, weird sonic blasts, total non-sense and strong irrationality meets. between neo-composed scores and prepared improvisation, the listener absorbs the unleashed raw beauty of the musical wrath. for more details, here is a good start: http://ooo.meovco.com Huong DoNa based in Hanoi, Huong DoNa is a regular face among the Hanoi sound art scene, often performing both solo and with collaborative projects. a member of Dom Dom collective, Huong has participated in many experiments along with other Vietnamese and international artists at Heritage Space, Goethe Institute, Phu Sa Lab, and elsewhere. her solo set was a highlight of the New Music Festival 2018 opening event, and she recently completed her solo marathon at A Space, performing a piece in which she encaged herself within a giant sound generating birdcage as a deep meditation on the relation between freedom and entrapment in the creation process of a work of art. curated by Alec Schachner (Scobi Wan / Ech Ech Studios) Friday, April 19 8:00 – 11:00 pm

title: artist presentation | Phùng Tiến Sơn
description: Hanoi-based artist Phùng Tiến Sơn will return to Saigon on Thursday April 18 for an artist presentation at MoT+++. the presentation marks the last week of his sound installation, Soundtracks , which is on view at MoT+++ until April 23, and will discuss the over six-month process behind the work. “we set foot in Vi Xuyen martyrs’ cemetery, which had 1768 (until July 2013) graves of the deceased from 1979 to the beginning of 1990, especially in 1984 and 1988. i recorded all the information about these 1768 people, which were etched on their tombstones. from the information i collected, i used Google Maps to draw connecting paths from their hometowns to Vi Xuyen (where they sacrificed their lives and their remaining corpses were concentrated). i separated these paths from the maps, for all that’s left are the cold and meaningless lines. in other words, we can only see 1768 winding strokes. is there anything left but 1768 routes which all lead to death at the same place?” – Phùng Tiến Sơn about the artist: Phùng Tiến Sơn is a multimedia visual artist and university dropout from Hanoi, Vietnam. music is a continuing feature of Sơn’s practice, and he cites sound and technology as important influences in his work. past exhibitions include group shows as an ongoing member of the Nhà Sàn Collective, the Hanoi Collective Orchestra in 2015 and 2016, and Utopia Land at Hanoi’s Heritage Space in 2017. in 2018, Sơn performed in Big Day of Performances – Happy Birthday Billas part of MoT+++’s six-month performance plus programme. Soundtracks was first exhibited in the Nhà Sàn Collective show Mise en Scene in 2016. Thursday, April 18 7pm

title: A. Farm | Krossing Over | In Situ: Tree of Life & Lub Qhov Rooj
description: A. Farm is proud to host two of the most outstanding performances at Krossing Over Arts Festival on April 7, 2019: Tree Of Life Choreographer: John Huy Trần & Nguyễn Vũ Hoàng Minh ÂMusic Composer: Benoît Bottex Dancers: UDG Vietnam Tree of Life is a tribute to life itself. As a tree grows old, its branches break then bears the seeds that contain its essence, through which it finds its way to life again, sprouting, endlessly growing and thriving in a way that seems immortal. Lub Qhov Rooj Choreographer: Nguyễn Phúc Hùng Music Composer: Hans Zimmer Dancers: Đặng Minh Hiển, Phan Thái Bình, Phan Tiểu Ly, Sùng A Lùng Poetry: Chinh Ba Visual Artist: Sandrine Llouquet Based on Sung A Lung’s life, Lub Qhov Rooj (“Door” in H’mong language) is about Lung’s struggle for being born into a man’s body. Like William Blake’s character Albion in the poem Four Zoas, Lung finds his four components, performed by four dancers. In this journey of collecting and reconciling the pieces, he discovers his true self. Saturday, April 7 6:30-7:00 pm: Reception 7:00-8:30 pm: Performance Location: A. Farm, 96/1 Tan Thoi Nhat 6, District 12, TP. HCMC, Vietnam Ticket: https://ticketbox.vn/event/krossing-over-in-situ-tree-of-life-lub-qhov-rooj-75059/52174 # capacity: Limited For more information, visit krossingover.com

title: +1 museum by any other name | flowers | Tran Minh Duc
description: the song ‘ Where have all the flowers gone?’ by Pete Seeger, heard on repeat as part of the flowers artwork, is listed as one of the top 20 most influencing political songs and an anti-war song from the 60s era. it is inspired by Mikhail Sholokhov’s novel The Quiet Flows the Don, and by three lines of an Ukrainian folk song: ‘where are the flowers, the girls have plucked them. where are the girls, they’ve all taken husbands. where are the men, they’re all in the army.’ composed of the word ‘flowers’ in rose coloured neon light writing mounted on the simple cardboard box of a popular Vietnamese instant noodle brand, the work exudes a gentle and tender air. the noodle box is nostalgic and humble, an allusion to childhood years in the subsidized period, when the artist’s mother sold coffee and food to earn a living in hard times. coming out from the box, a small set of headphones play the song like an echo; its sound creating a heady audio essence that emanates from within. together, in a modest ode, these elements accentuate the interchange between the physical and intangible factors at play in the work, and slowly unfurl the complex notions of beauty and peace. about the artist: Tran Minh Duc (b.1982, Vietnam) is a visual artist based in Ho Chi Minh City, Vietnam working with the mediums of performance, photography, collage and installation. through a multidisciplinary approach, Duc seeks to explore collective memory and cultural archives by investigating historical narratives, the effects of colonialism and imperialism, and the lasting impacts of war and migration. he is interested in the interactions between the collective and individual, and the local and the foreign; his work forms a personal interrogation of what it means to be Vietnamese in the intricate fabric of contemporaneity. Duc graduated with a BA in painting from the College of Culture and Arts of Ho Chi Minh City, and has since exhibited widely in Vietnam and internationally. in 2017, he received a fellowship from the Asian Cultural Council to be in residence at Art in General in New York City, USA. his artwork flowers was previously exhibited with MoT+++ in 2015, and is part of the Post Vidai, a Vietnamese Art Collection. April 2019 – April 2020 location: Padma de Fleur, 55/6 Le Thi Hong Gam, p. Nguyen Thai Binh, D1, HCMC, Vietnam hours: Tue – Sun from 9am til 10pm Padma de Fleur flowers is on loan from Post Vidai collection

title: Trần Minh Đức
description: Trần Minh Đức is a Vietnamese artist whose work flowers was acquired by the +1 museum by any other name collection at MoT+++. the piece centres on the song 'Where have all the flowers gone?' by Pete Seeger, exploring themes of anti-war, memory, and the passage of time.

description: the song ‘ Where have all the flowers gone?’ by Pete Seeger, heard on repeat as part of the flowers artwork, is listed as one of the top 20 most influencing political songs and an anti-war song from the 60s era. it is inspired by Mikhail Sholokhov’s novel The Quiet Flows the Don, and by three lines of an Ukrainian folk song: ‘where are the flowers, the girls have plucked them. where are the girls, they’ve all taken husbands. where are the men, they’re all in the army.’ composed of the word ‘flowers’ in rose coloured neon light writing mounted on the simple cardboard box of a popular Vietnamese instant noodle brand, the work exudes a gentle and tender air. the noodle box is nostalgic and humble, an allusion to childhood years in the subsidized period, when the artist’s mother sold coffee and food to earn a living in hard times. coming out from the box, a small set of headphones play the song like an echo; its sound creating a heady audio essence that emanates from within. together, in a modest ode, these elements accentuate the interchange between the physical and intangible factors at play in the work, and slowly unfurl the complex notions of beauty and peace. about the artist: Tran Minh Duc (b.1982, Vietnam) is a visual artist based in Ho Chi Minh City, Vietnam working with the mediums of performance, photography, collage and installation. through a multidisciplinary approach, Duc seeks to explore collective memory and cultural archives by investigating historical narratives, the effects of colonialism and imperialism, and the lasting impacts of war and migration. he is interested in the interactions between the collective and individual, and the local and the foreign; his work forms a personal interrogation of what it means to be Vietnamese in the intricate fabric of contemporaneity. vnTitle: Những bông hoa | Trần Minh Đức
vnDescription: bài hát Tất cả những bông hoa đã biến mất ở đâu? bởi Pete Seeger, được nghe lặp lại như một phần của tác phẩm nghệ thuật Những bông hoa, được liệt kê là một trong 20 bài hát chính trị có ảnh hưởng nhất và một bài hát phản chiến từ thời 60. bài hát lấy cảm hứng từ tiểu thuyết Sông Đông êm đềm bởi Mikhail Sholokhov, và bởi ba dòng của một bài hát dân gian Ucraina: ‘Những bông hoa ở đâu, các cô gái đã nhổ chúng. các cô gái ở đâu, họ đều lấy chồng. những người đàn ông ở đâu, tất cả bọn họ ở trong quân đội. được làm từ chữ ‘Những bông hoa’, chữ viết bằng đèn neon màu hồng gắn trên hộp các tông đơn giản của một thương hiệu mì ăn liền nổi tiếng của Việt Nam, tác phẩm toát lên một không khí nhẹ nhàng và dịu dàng. hộp mì hoài cổ và khiêm nhường, ám chỉ những năm tháng tuổi thơ trong thời kỳ bao cấp, khi mẹ của nghệ sĩ bán cà phê và thức ăn để kiếm sống trong thời kỳ khó khăn. đi ra từ trong hộp, một bộ tai nghe nhỏ phát bài hát như tiếng vang; âm thanh của nó tạo ra một chất âm thanh toả ra từ bên trong. cùng nhau, trong một bài ca khiêm tốn, những yếu tố này làm bật lên sự trao đổi giữa các yếu tố vật lý và phi vật thể trong tác phẩm, và dần dần mở ra những khái niệm phức tạp về vẻ đẹp và hòa bình. về nghệ sĩ: Trần Minh Đức (b.1982, Việt Nam) là một nghệ sĩ thị giác từ Thành phố Hồ Chí Minh, Việt Nam làm việc với các phương tiện trình diễn, chụp ảnh, cắt dán và sắp đặt. thông qua cách tiếp cận đa ngành, Đức tìm cách khám phá ký ức tập thể và tài liệu lưu trữ văn hóa bằng cách điều tra các câu chuyện lịch sử, tác động của chủ nghĩa thực dân và chủ nghĩa đế quốc, và các tác động lâu dài của chiến tranh và di cư. anh quan tâm đến sự tương tác giữa tập thể và cá nhân, và địa phương và nước ngoài; công việc của anh ta hình thành một cuộc thẩm vấn cá nhân về ý nghĩa của việc trở thành người Việt Nam trong cấu trúc phức tạp của tính đương thời. Đức tốt nghiệp cử nhân ngành hội họa tại Đại học Văn hóa Nghệ thuật Thành phố Hồ Chí Minh, và từ đó đã được triển lãm rộng rãi ở Việt Nam và quốc tế. vào năm 2017, anh nhận được học bổng từ Hội đồng văn hóa châu Á để lưu trú tại Art in General ở thành phố New York, Hoa Kỳ. những tác phẩm nghệ thuật hoa của anh trước đây đã được trưng bày với MoT +++ vào năm 2015 và là một phần của Post Vidai, Bộ sưu tập nghệ thuật Việt Nam. Tháng 4 năm 2019 – Tháng 4 năm 2020 địa điểm: Padma de Fleur, 55/6 Lê Thị Hồng Gấm, đường Nguyễn Thái Bình, quận 1, TP HCM, Việt Nam giờ mở cửa: Thứ ba – CN từ 9h sáng đến 10h tối Padma de Fleur Những bông hoa được cho mượn từ bộ sưu tập Post Vidai Những bông hoa | Trần Minh Đức – MoT+++ programs collective MoTsound A. Farm about us Những bông hoa | Trần Minh Đức

title: Matthew Brannon
description: Matthew Brannon has long been recognized not only for his wit and literary sensibility but also for the precision with which he approaches his chosen mediums. He is perhaps best known for his radical approach to printmaking, which, contrary to traditional usage, frequently involves the elaborate production of unique artworks. The vocabulary and voice developed in the prints–arch and erudite, with a sharply psychoanalytic bent–have provided the center for an expanding world of objects and narratives that also includes painting, sculpture, video, and installation. Learn more about his works: matthewbrannon.com Matthew Brannon – MoT+++ A. Farm International Art Residency a Saigon-based art residency, in partnership with the Goethe-Institut Ho Chi Minh City, and support from Amanaki Thao Dien

title: Scott Anderson
description: Scott Anderson is an artist based in Australia. His art practice is concerned with exploring painting, collage and sculpture, working collectively as a hybridised practice. Through his studio work Scott’s research investigates architecture, place/non-place, transition/flux, construction/deconstruction and how cultural identities can influence one’s understanding of place. When creating work, Scott will often use locally reclaimed materials and is not concerned with a preconceived outcome but prefers to develop “a call and response” methodology, allowing each piece to develop organically, taking on a life of its own. Scott Anderson – MoT+++ A. Farm International Art Residency a Saigon-based art residency, in partnership with the Goethe-Institut Ho Chi Minh City, and support from Amanaki Thao Dien

title: MoT sound #9 | aConvergence
description: MoT sound event series will re-open for 2019 hosting a night of exploratory sounds with three acts from Vietnam and abroad. ever-encouraging forays into the new, this edition of MoT will feature solo performances from Saigon’s own Jung Buffalo the under underground kid wonder, Linhhafornow back from touring Asia to visit us from Hanoi and Llama / Olo, a very special guest duo from Switzerland. Jung Buffalo is one of the many aliases of Đỗ Tấn Sĩ, b. 1997, a musician / music producer from Ho Chi Minh City, Vietnam. as Si’s ongoing solo project, Jung Buffalo’s work has explored many forms & genres from ambient noisescapes to glitch trap, minimal acoustic-electric excursions, dronestep, killer-barber-hop, contemporary classical. Si is also the founding member of Ran Cap Duoi Collective . he sometimes wears hats. reaching deep into her soul LinhHafornow transports audiences through wondrous, uplifting and reflective emotional moments. she blends ambient harmonies, traditional instruments and soaring vocals into smooth arrangements of original songs. with a ‘loopstation’, an electronic device more popular with musicians from outside the ambient scene, she uses multiple microphones to capture her voice and a variety of traditional instruments. float through an imagined dreamscape… guided by Linh Hà … for now. check SoundCloud and Vimeo for more on LinhHafornow. soundcloud.com/linhha vimeo.com/user77196635 LLAMA / OLO is an encounter between two activists of the underground, on one side Didier Severin, leader of the terrifying Knut who has for 18 years plowed deep the wild lands of metal across the planet, and on the other the adventurous Loïc Grobéty who has dragged his hump from black metal to tight rock via contemporary music. these 2 protagonists crush the matter, violent it and push the sound in its last retrenchments. the result is dark but cinematographic, powerful and slow. sound penetrates and embraces you to give you a unique sensory experience. line up : Didier Séverin – synthé modulaire Loïc Grobéty – électrique corde piano+électronique curated by Alec Schachner (Scobi Wan / Ech Ech Studios) Tuesday, March 19 7:00 – 10:00 pm
vnDescription: chuỗi sự kiện âm thanh MoT sẽ mở lại cho năm 2019 bằng một đêm khám phá âm thanh với ba nhóm nhạc tới từ Việt Nam và nước ngoài. hướng tới những đột phá mới, dịp này MoT âm thanh đưa tới màn trình diễn solo bởi cậu bé kỳ diệu của giới underground Jung Buffalo từ Sài Gòn, Linhhafornow tới từ Hà Nội, trở về từ chuyến lưu diễn châu Á để ghé thăm chúng tôi và Llama / Olo, một bộ đôi rất đặc biệt đến từ Thụy sĩ. Jung Buffalo là một trong nhiều bí danh của Đỗ Tấn Sĩ, sn.1997, một nhạc sĩ / nhà sản xuất âm nhạc từ thành phố Hồ Chí Minh, Việt Nam. như là một dự án solo tiếp diễn của Sĩ, các sản phẩm của Jung Buffalo khám phá nhiều hình thức & amp; từ các thể loại như ambient noisescapes đến glitch trap, minimal acoustic-electric excursions, dronestep, killer-barber-hop, contemporary classical. Sĩ cũng là thành viên sáng lập của nhóm Rắn Cạp Đuôi thỉnh thoảng anh đội mũ. đi sâu vào tâm hồn cô ấy LinhHafornow chuyển tiếp tới khán giả những khoảnh khắc cảm xúc kỳ diệu, bay bổng và phản ảnh. cô pha trộn hài hòa giữa môi trường xung quanh, nhạc cụ truyền thống và giọng hát cao vút để tạo thành sự thoả hiệp uyển chuyển với các bài nhạc nguyên bản. cùng với ‘loopstation’, một thiết bị điện tử phổ biến hơn đối với các nhạc sĩ từ bên ngoài dòng ambient, cô dùng nhiều micro để nắm bắt giọng hát và các loại nhạc cụ truyền thống. bồng bềnh trên một khung cảnh mộng tưởng tưởng tượng được dẫn dắt bởi Linh Hà..tạm thời là thế. tham khảo SoundCloud và Vimeo để biết thêm về LinhHafornow. soundcloud.com/linhha vimeo.com/user77196635 LLAMA / OLO là cuộc gặp gỡ giữa hai nhà hoạt động của giới underground, một mặt Didier Severin, lãnh đạo của Knut tồi tệ đã có 18 năm cày sâu trong vùng hoang dã bên mảng kim loại trên khắp hành tinh và mặt khác Loïc Grobéty thích phiêu lưu, người đã thông qua âm nhạc đương đại để kéo cái bướu của mình từ black metal tới tight rock. 2 nhân vật chủ đạo này nghiền nát vật chất, bạo lực chúng và đẩy âm thanh đến thành luỹ cuối cùng của nó. kết quả thì tối nhưng đầy tính điện ảnh, hùng cường và chậm rãi. âm thanh thâm nhập và bao vây lấy bạn đưa tới bạn một trải nghiệm độc đáo. line up: Didier Séverin – synthé modulaire Loïc Grobéty – électrique corde piano+électronique giám tuyển bởi Alec Schachner (Scobi Wan / Ech Ech Studios) thứ ba, 19 tháng 03 7:00 – 10:00 pm địa điểm: Saigon Domaine, Tầng trệt, 1057 Bình Quoi, Phường 28, quận Bình Thạnh, thành phố Hồ Chí Minh. vé vào cửa: 100.000 đ MoT sound #9 | aConvergence – MoT+++ programs collective MoTsound A. Farm about us MoT sound #9 | aConvergence

title: MoT sound #9 | aConvergence
description: chuỗi sự kiện âm thanh MoT sẽ mở lại cho năm 2019 bằng một đêm khám phá âm thanh với ba nhóm nhạc tới từ Việt Nam và nước ngoài. hướng tới những đột phá mới, dịp này MoT âm thanh đưa tới màn trình diễn solo bởi cậu bé kỳ diệu của giới underground Jung Buffalo từ Sài Gòn, Linhhafornow tới từ Hà Nội, trở về từ chuyến lưu diễn châu Á để ghé thăm chúng tôi và Llama / Olo, một bộ đôi rất đặc biệt đến từ Thụy sĩ. title: Wu Chi-Tsung
description: Wu Chi-Tsung is a Taiwanese visual artist based in Taipei. His practice spans photography, video, and installation, with his experimental Cyano-Collage series exploring the intersection of traditional Chinese painting aesthetics and contemporary photographic processes.

title: Soundtracks | Ký Âm | Phùng Tiến Sơn
description: Soundtracks a sound installation by Phùng Tiến Sơn from February 23rd to April 23rd 2019 MoT+++ will present the sound installation Soundtracks from the artist Phùng Tiến Sơn. the work builds on the artist’s history as a musical composer, as well as his interest in technology thanks to its use of commonly found and customised software. through the digital manipulation of sound, the artist forms a space for quiet reflection from his audience, leaving them to contemplate, among other things, the overarching sense of futility with which the work engages. “we set foot in Vi Xuyen martyrs’ cemetery, which had 1768 (until July 2013) graves of the deceased from 1979 to the beginning of 1990, especially in 1984 and 1988. i recorded all the information about these 1768 people, which were etched on their tombstones. from the information i collected, i used Google Maps to draw connecting paths from their hometowns to Vi Xuyen (where they sacrificed their lives and their remaining corpses were concentrated). i separated these paths from the maps, for all that’s left are the cold and meaningless lines. in other words, we can only see 1768 winding strokes. is there anything left but 1768 routes which all lead to death at the same place in North Vietnam?” – Phùng Tiến Sơn the installation will take place alongside multiple MoT sound events in the months of March and April 2019 that focus on the element of sound across as range of artistic practices. MoT sound is an experimental project that gives artists the freedom to explore the textural and performative aspects of their work, unfettered by genre-based preconditions. it is an investigation into and out from our daily auditory experiences, that challenges our relation to the audible world and how that relationship might change when experienced collectively. about the artist: Phùng Tiến Sơn is a multimedia visual artist and university dropout from Hanoi, Vietnam. music is a continuing feature of Sơn’s practice, and he cites sound and technology as important influences in his work. past exhibitions include group shows as an ongoing member of the Nhà Sàn Collective, the Hanoi Collective Orchestra in 2015 and 2016, and Utopia Land at Hanoi’s Heritage Space in 2017. in 2018, Sơn performed in Big Day of Performances – Happy Birthday Bill as part of MoT+++’s six-month performance plus programme. Soundtracks was first exhibited in the Nhà Sàn Collective show Mise en Scene in 2016. February 23 – April 23, 2019 opening: Saturday February 23, 2019 from 6:00pm – 9:00pm

title: Phùng Tiến Sơn
description: a Hanoi-based artist whose practice engages with music composition and technology. he presented Soundtracks (Ký Âm), a sound installation at MoT+++ running from February to April 2019, building on his history as a musical composer and his interest in the digital manipulation of sound.

title: A. Farm | In Progress #2
description: A. Farm would like to invite you to IN PROGRESS #2, an open-studio event introducing the works of our Season 1 artists Tuyen Nguyen, Alisa Chunchue, Lem Trag, Lananh Le, and Karen Thao La, Scott Anderson and Cora & Tilman. Occurring each Season, IN PROGRESS engages with the development of the artist’s practice rather than their final product. By opening the artist’s studio, both artist and audience are provided a moment for reflection and opportunity to engage in a wider dialogue surrounding the artist’s work. Audiences are invited from 4-7pm on Sunday 17th February to see A. Farm’s Season 1 artists’ work-in-progress. Starting at 5pm, each artist will make a short presentation in their studio where they will discuss their work and progression of ideas. This is also the opportunity to welcome you all back to our space, introduce our amazing artists, and spend the afternoon hanging out and sipping on beers and coconuts in the courtyard. Don’t make you Sunday a bore, and come join us for an afternoon of art and conversation!

title: Cora von Zezschwitz & Tilman Hoepfl
description: Cora von Zezschwitz & Tilman Hoepfl both live and work in France. Besides pursuing their own art practice, a.k.a. photography, video, installation and painting, sculpture, and installation, both artists work since 2016 together on collaborative projects exploring the idea of social sculpture. Based on modular structures and the idea of the architecture of additions, they create physical platforms for dialogue and discussion, inspiring towards the idea of communal interaction and reflection, exploration and re-/construction, cultural exchange and togetherness. Cora von Zezschwitz & Tilman Hoepfl – MoT+++ A. Farm International Art Residency a Saigon-based art residency, in partnership with the Goethe-Institut Ho Chi Minh City, and support from Amanaki Thao Dien

title: artist talk | collaborative performance | Singapore
description: introduction | participants artists talk is a collaborative ongoing performance piece by artists Aliansyah Caniago and Cam Xanh, conceived during Alinsayah’s residency at MoT+++. it was first performed in March 2018 in Ho Chi Minh City, Vietnam as part of performance plus, a six-month performance art programme at MoT+++ running from March to August 2018. spreading globally, like the phenomenon from which its name derives, artists talk was conceived to be an ongoing series of utterances, with one performance per city worldwide. by gathering artists in a room to talk but removing their audience, the performance questions the very concept of the “artist talk”, and the obtuse nature of “talk” itself. in requiring artists to continually vocalise their internal dialogues, it turns the thought inside out, and asks whether they really have something to say or if it’s just talking for the sake of talking. a three-channel video, with footage of previous talk, was installed in the room creating a clamour of constant noise. the multiple harmonies and dissonances of audible layers formed from this orchestra of incessant talk giving artists a platform to speak freely from cultural or political censorship, whilst simultaneously eradicating the possibility for them to be heard. the second iteration of artists talk took place in Singapore as part of the S.E.A. Focus art fair. artists working in any discipline were invited to join Aliansyah Caniago, Cam Xanh and the MoT+++ collective, on Friday, January 25 2019 from 6:30 to 8:30pm. replicating the first performance, participants were encouraged to talk simultaneously for two hours, and follow whatever vocal urges emerged. before entering, they were given a printed copy of the freedom of expression manifesto written by Cam Xanh, which set out a number of performance directives dictating their behaviour once inside the room. the manifesto inhibited artists from making eye contact or touching each other, as well as direct responses, although mimicry and provocation of other artists was allowed. most importantly, participants were required to stay inside the locked room for the entire two hours of the performance. nine participants took part in the performance: four MoT+++ collective, four guest artists, including an art technician-cum-artist, and one curator. only participants were allowed in the room, with audience members restricted to watching through eye-level windows, unable to distinguish the murmurings inside. the purpose-built room was located adjacent to the S.E.A. Spotlight Talks, where industry and artists panels and talks were staged throughout the fair from January 24-27. in an echo of the initial performance, some artists began to draw with charcoal on the walls. this action was extended in the performance’s second occurance to drawing on the windows with lipstick and markers also. artists used the paper articulations of the freedom of expression manifesto to cover the windows, further nullifying the

title: Tuan Mami
description: tuan mami is a conceptual artist working in performance, alongside other media. his work as a performance artist developed alongside a long engagement with the form. he gave an artist presentation as part of performance plus 2019 at MoT+++, sharing his experiences in performance art alongside cam xanh.

title: Scott Farrand
description: Scott is an emerging artist from Adelaide in South Australia. Born in 1954 he has a Bachelor of Visual Art and Design from the Adelaide College of the Arts majoring in Sculpture. He has had several joint exhibitions in his home state; most notably as a winner in the Waterhouse Natural Science Art Prize. His painting and sculptural art works are expressions of an ongoing interest in the values that underpin and determine the nature of the societies in which we live. His work explores the relationship we have with materialism and its many manifestations; consumerism, the search for happiness, social inclusion, poverty, political spin, climate change and the environment. Scott works with a wide range of materials such as LED lights, recycled plastics and discarded objects but always keeps the joy of making as the central component. Scott Farrand – MoT+++ A. Farm International Art Residency a Saigon-based art residency, in partnership with the Goethe-Institut Ho Chi Minh City, and support from Amanaki Thao Dien

title: Arnont Nongyao
description: Arnont Nongyao is a Thai artist based in Chiang Mai, Thailand working in sound, video, and installation. his primary research interest is sound, with a concentration on vibration, field recording, and acoustic phenomena. he participated in MoT sound #10 aNoise and MoT sound #24.

title: mountains and rivers of the.. | Bang Nhat Linh & Wu Chi-Tsung
description: mountains and rivers of the.. Bang Nhat Linh & Wu Chi-Tsung opening: Friday, December 14, 2018 @ 6pm on view: December 14, 2018 – February 1, 2019 following a two-month residency at MoT+++ , Wu Chi-Tsung’s studio will be opened to the public and placed in conversation with the installation work ‘Summer Grasses’ by Bang Nhat Linh. visitors are welcome to MoT+++ to see the interactions between works from the two artists. originating from Hanoi and Taipei respectively, Bang Nhat Linh and Wu Chi-Tsung’s artworks sensitively respond to centuries of geopolitical tensions in the region, as well as the personal histories of each of the artists. ‘Summer Grasses’ by Bang Nhat Linh is a commission by the Nguyen Art Foundation. about Bang Nhat Linh: Bang Nhat Linh was born in 1983 in Hanoi. Working primarily as a visual artist, Bang Nhat Linh is also known as a collector of war memorabilia—an interest that has formed the basis for earlier works such as The Vacant Chair, which showed previously at MoT+++ in 2015 as well as The Mori Art Museum, Tokyo as part of the 2017 ‘SUNSHOWER: Contemporary Art from Southeast Asia 1980s to Now’ show. Bang Nhat Linh cites human beings as a primary concern and inspiration for his artistic practice. His work carries themes of memory, history, submerged psychological spaces, and oblivion. about Wu Chi-Tsung: Wu Chi-Tsung was born in 1981 in Taipei. Wu received his Bachelor of Fine Arts from the Taipei National University of the Arts in 2004. He currently lives and works in Taipei, Taiwan and Berlin, Germany. His work, in which he devotes great attention to the methods used in producing and interpreting images, spans across different media, including photography, video, installation art, painting, and set design. He combines traditions and contemporary art forms from the East and the West. Daily objects and phenomena are of great inspiration for his work, and what he transforms into poetic imagery. Wu Chi-Tsung received the top prize of the Taipei Arts Award (2003), short-listed for the “Artes Mundi” (2006), received “WRO Media Art Biannual” (2013) – Award of Critics and Editors of Art Magazines”, and short-listed for the “Prudential Eye Awards” (2015). download the booklet: mountains and rivers of the.. | Bang Nhat Ninh & Wu Chi-Tsung [/tab] [/tabgroup]

title: A. Farm | Buy . Bay . Buy
description: A. Farm presents bye, bay, buy, an end-of-year celebration concluding the very first residency experiments at a. farm. as this cycle comes to an end, season 0 artists maung day, trâm lương, nguyễn kim duy, phương giò, john edmond smyth (ed smyth), lại diệu hà, luca lum, latthapon korkiatarkul, and cian duggan present the culmination of their residency work. bye . bay . buy also features photographic prints by maxime brygo of southern vietnamese landscapes and monuments, the result of a three-month residency at a. farm. this is an opportunity to say farewell to season 0 artists and welcome season 1 arrivals. exhibition re-opens monday december 10 and remains on view for two weeks. location: A. Farm, 96/1 tan thoi nhat 6, district 12, ho chi minh city. free entrance.
vnDescription: A. Farm hân hạnh mời bạn đến bye, bay, buy, lễ ăn mừng cuối năm đánh dấu thời điểm những thử nghiệm trong mùa lưu trú đầu tiên đi tới kết thúc. nghệ sĩ 'mùa 0' maung day, trâm lương, nguyễn kim duy, phương giò, ed smyth, lại diệu hà, luca lum, latthapon korkiatarkul và cian duggan trình bày tác phẩm trong khuôn khổ sự kiện. triển lãm cũng giới thiệu tác phẩm ảnh của maxime brygo về cảnh quan và di tích miền nam việt nam. địa điểm: A. Farm, 96/1 tân thới nhất 6, quận 12, tp.hcm. vào cửa miễn phí.

title: exchange | Le Quoc Thanh to Seoul
description: le quoc thanh took part in a residency exchange with baik art in seoul, south korea, as part of MoT+++'s ongoing exchange collaboration with baik art. le quoc thanh was stationed at baik art's space in seoul from november 1 to december 1, 2018.

title: Latthapon Korkiatarkul
description: Latthapon Korkiatarkul has exhibited extensively in Southeast Asia since he graduated with his BFA in Fine Arts in 2010 and had his first solo show, (Un)Composition, in 2017. Latthapon’s work often revolves around the desacralization of everyday and art objects, highlighting the importance of mental processes and humour in his practice. Latthapon Korkiatarkul – MoT+++ A. Farm International Art Residency a Saigon-based art residency, in partnership with the Goethe-Institut Ho Chi Minh City, and support from Amanaki Thao Dien

title: Residency and Open Studio | Wu Chi-Tsung
description: From October to December 2018, the MoT+++ space was transformed into a workspace for visual artist Wu Chi-Tsung, who developed new artworks in his experimental photography series Cyano-Collages.

title: Luca Lum
description: Luca Lum is an artist and writer. She works across and between fiction, performance, mixed-media installation, and forms of mediation. She is also the co-founder of soft/WALL/studs, a studio-library-project space in Singapore. Learn more about her works: lucaelum.com Luca Lum – MoT+++ A. Farm International Art Residency a Saigon-based art residency, in partnership with the Goethe-Institut Ho Chi Minh City, and support from Amanaki Thao Dien

title: Nguyen Duc Phuong
description: Nguyen Duc Phuong uses natural colour pigments originating from soil, flowers and plants for his paintings on ‘dzó’ paper (traditional handmade paper from northern Vietnam), creating works that are delicate and full of life. Nguyen Duc Phuong – MoT+++ A. Farm International Art Residency a Saigon-based art residency, in partnership with the Goethe-Institut Ho Chi Minh City, and support from Amanaki Thao Dien

title: Cian Duggan
description: Cian Duggan was brought up on skate culture during his teenage years. Drawing inspiration from its bootleg and DIY culture, he mixes it up with an interest in industrial materials and design, combining organic yet alien shapes with human and figurative elements. Most of his work is on the walls and streets of places he likes to visit. Learn more about his works: cian-duggan.com Cian Duggan – MoT+++ A. Farm International Art Residency a Saigon-based art residency, in partnership with the Goethe-Institut Ho Chi Minh City, and support from Amanaki Thao Dien

title: John Edmond Smyth
description: John Edmond Smyth is interested in ideas of visual noise and the degradation of form and will explore these themes through his drawing practice. John Edmond Smyth – MoT+++ A. Farm International Art Residency a Saigon-based art residency, in partnership with the Goethe-Institut Ho Chi Minh City, and support from Amanaki Thao Dien

title: Kim Duy
description: Kim Duy’s multidisciplinary practice questions the changing condition of time and space in art and language, the reading of history through visual art and the performative nature existing behind still images. Kim Duy – MoT+++ A. Farm International Art Residency a Saigon-based art residency, in partnership with the Goethe-Institut Ho Chi Minh City, and support from Amanaki Thao Dien

title: Tram Luong
description: Tram Luong is a trained filmmaker and visual anthropologist. Her work examines the interrelation between collective memories, historical trauma, and cultural representation. Tram Luong – MoT+++ A. Farm International Art Residency a Saigon-based art residency, in partnership with the Goethe-Institut Ho Chi Minh City, and support from Amanaki Thao Dien

title: entrances | Cian Duggan
description: “entrances’” by Cian Duggan opened as MoT+++ third +1 museum by any other name on Friday November 23, 2018. as pair of works on a busy highway in Saigon one ‘entrance’ had already collapsed by the time of the opening. the second ‘entrance’ closed unfortunately the next day. part 2 “exit” will appear at an unforseen time in the future. sent floating into the sea at Hue, anyone that ‘entered’ via any one of Duggan’s works could eventually ‘exit’ in an unknown place and at an unknown time. Duggan’s abstract forms appear otherworldly, they hover with a static tension that animates them, as extra-terrestrial life forms, or portals to another dimension. yet they coexist with their surroundings, rather than overtake it. somewhere between memory and imagination, primitive and futuristic, organic and digital.. the evocation of the familiar rendered unfamiliar lending the forms their oddness. part 1 of Duggan’s +1 museum by any other name materialises as multiple “entrances”; gateways suggesting disruptive possibilities to the linearity of time and space. their incongruity makes them strangely recognisable as they allude to the many other similar “entrances” hidden in plain sight amongst Saigon’s streets. although there is the possibility that old entrances may close, consumed by the rapidly developing urban environment, Duggan’s compulsion to paint means that new entrances are continually, if only briefly, emerging, waiting to be found. the “entrances” diptych is part 1 of Duggan’s work for MoT+++’s +1 museum by any other name. part 2 “exit” is a singular piece planned for an unforeseen time in the future—a yet to be realised second half of the work that will join other floating artworks in the sea off the coast of Hue, in a venture organised by fellow +1 museum artist Dao Tung. one year after its construction, part 2 will be untethered and released into the ocean creating an unknowable “exit”, drifting in both time and space. entrances | Cian Duggan – MoT+++ programs collective MoTsound A.
